THOUGHT:
The search returned the exact paper I queried for: "WILDS: A Benchmark of in-the-Wild Distribution Shifts" (Koh et al., ICML 2020). This paper is highly relevant as it provides a unified benchmark specifically designed to evaluate distribution shifts across diverse real-world applications. It directly addresses Category 3 (Datasets/Benchmarks) by introducing WILDS, and Category 4 (Comparing Findings) by demonstrating substantial performance drops of baseline models when evaluated on out-of-distribution data compared to standard i.i.d. splits. It will be cited in the Related Work or Introduction to highlight the evaluation mismatch between academic benchmarks and real-world deployment, motivating the need for robust evaluation protocols.
